characters.name: Bacon Hair
characters.description: He works at the factory by the river. He helps roll big oil drums for the machines.
alibis_innocent: A video shows a taller person moving the drums. His shift had already ended.
His badge clocked him at the dock when the oil hit the river. A camera pointed the wrong way.
Oil on the drum handles does not match his gloves. He was fixing a machine inside.
alibis_guilty: Two drums said recycled, but no truck ever picked them up. Scrape marks by the drain fit his cart.
He complained about drum fees at work that day. The river oil matches the oil he uses every day.
A night video shows gloves like his right after the drums moved. Only his crew wears those gloves.
characters.suspicious_detail: Workers say his work cart was seen rolling near the river drain.
characters.innocent_explanation: The river oil came from factory drums. The drum mover in the video is not him.
characters.culprit_explanation: He poured machine oil from the drums into the drain. That drain pipe leads straight into the river.

characters.name: Ballerina Cappuccina
characters.description: She runs a small cafe on the river walk. She cleans dishes and floors with soap and spray bottles.
alibis_innocent: She drove away before anyone moved the drums. Her night trash was only cups and food scraps.
She was home when factory cameras filmed the drums rolling.
Inspectors only allow food-safe cleaners in her shop. None of those match the thick river oil.
alibis_guilty: Workers saw large cleaner jugs behind her cafe after closing. The labels match heavy grease cleaner in the gutter.
Empty jugs with her logo were found beside the drain.
Pour times match rainy nights. Her phone stayed behind the shop on those same nights.
characters.suspicious_detail: Empty jugs with her cafe logo were found right beside the drain.
characters.innocent_explanation: The river stain is thick factory oil. Her cafe only uses dish soap and food-safe cleaners.
characters.culprit_explanation: She poured old grease cleaner behind the cafe. Rain washed the oily mix straight into the river.

characters.name: Tung Tung Tung Sahur
characters.description: He runs the factory night shift by the river. He signs papers when oil trucks leave the plant.
alibis_innocent: His van was empty when he stopped by the drain. His papers show no drums on board.
Plant cameras place him on the repair floor during the drum video.
His key card never unlocked the room where the drums are stored.
alibis_guilty: He borrowed an orange jacket from the locker room. Fibers on the grate match that jacket.
He closed a repair ticket early. His phone pinged by the drain at the same time.
The forms he signed do not match real pickups. The recycling company never got the drums.
characters.suspicious_detail: Orange jacket fibers from his locker were snagged on the drain grate.
characters.innocent_explanation: The worker in the video wears orange work clothes. He wore a yellow supervisor vest on camera that night.
characters.culprit_explanation: He signed fake papers so oil could leave the plant in secret. That oil went into the river, not recycling.

characters.name: Roblox Noob
characters.description: He hands out maps at the museum by the river. He does not work inside the oil factory.
alibis_innocent: He led a kids scavenger hunt before the drums moved.
His badge only opens public museum doors. He never entered the factory after seven p.m.
He signed out at the front desk hours before the drum video time.
alibis_guilty: His volunteer vest was found crumpled by the drain.
He asked staff how to open the service gate.
Drum labels were in his backpack. Oil smears on the bag matched the river.
characters.suspicious_detail: His volunteer vest was found crumpled on the ground by the drain.
characters.innocent_explanation: The river oil came from factory drums. He has no keys to the drum storage room.
characters.culprit_explanation: He borrowed a worker badge to enter the locked drum area. He moved drums to the drain to skip safe removal fees.

characters.name: Roblox Guest
characters.description: He visits on a one-day paper guest pass. He likes to take photos on the river path.
alibis_innocent: Front desk records show he left before the drums moved.
River cameras never show him at the drain that night.
His phone stayed across town until morning.
alibis_guilty: His guest pass appears in a clip near the drums.
He walked in through a side door someone left open.
He texted a friend asking where the barrels go.
characters.suspicious_detail: His guest pass shows up in a clip filmed very close to the oil drums.
characters.innocent_explanation: The oil came from factory drums at night. He had already left downtown in a rideshare.
characters.culprit_explanation: He returned in work clothes and moved the oil drums. He used his visitor pass as a cover story to get inside.

characters.name: Baconette Hair
characters.description: She sells candy and drinks from a cart on the river path. Her cart is supposed to carry food only.
alibis_innocent: She packed her cart away before the drums ever moved.
Health inspectors say she may only keep food in her cart.
Her wristband cannot open the plant gate.
alibis_guilty: Hidden jugs under her cart bottom held the same oil as the river.
A witness saw her cart by the grate after hours.
Glue on her gloves matched sealant from the drum caps.
characters.suspicious_detail: Hidden jugs under her cart liner held the same oil found in the river.
characters.innocent_explanation: The river shows thick factory machine oil. Her cart only holds snacks and drinks.
characters.culprit_explanation: She hid small oil bottles under the cart lining. She poured them down the drain while restocking at night.

characters.name: Peeley
characters.description: He wears a banana costume for kids at the museum. The suit is big, puffy, and hard to miss.
alibis_innocent: He was still in the suit when the drums moved on camera.
The drain door stayed locked until staff left for the night.
Coworkers saw him in the dressing room at the key time.
alibis_guilty: Video shows him removing the suit and wearing orange coveralls.
Costume fluff was found on a drum at the grate.
He posted online from the alley behind the museum.
characters.suspicious_detail: Yellow costume fluff was stuck to an oil drum by the river grate.
characters.innocent_explanation: The video shows a thin worker in coveralls. A banana suit is too bulky to roll drums.
characters.culprit_explanation: He took off the suit and put on stolen work clothes. Then he rolled oil drums to the drain.

characters.description: He films the river for the city cleanup team. His tripod legs have sticky grease from outdoor work.
alibis_innocent: His camera stayed pointed upriver during the drum video.
His full recording shows no long gap for a factory trip.
Plant security never scanned his badge at the drums.
alibis_guilty: Oily rags in his camera bag match the river oil.
His tripod stood in the mud right at the grate.
He tossed an oil bottle behind his case. The oil matched the factory blend.
characters.suspicious_detail: Oily rags from his camera bag tested the same as the river oil.
characters.innocent_explanation: The river oil came straight from factory drums. He was filming upriver when those drums moved.
characters.culprit_explanation: He poured extra machine oil from a small can near his gear into the drain. It matched the factory oil type.

characters.name: Roblox Builder
characters.description: He helps kids build projects at the museum by the river. His demo table uses tiny oil tubes to show motion.
alibis_innocent: He locked the tool room and stayed in the workshop on video.
His key card never opens the room where waste is stored.
Parents watched him on a live stream during the drum video.
alibis_guilty: Oil from his project table matches the oil in the river.
He opened a tunnel door that leads near the grate.
Empty oil tubes with his name were in his locker.
characters.suspicious_detail: Empty oil tubes with his name turned up inside his museum locker.
characters.innocent_explanation: The spill came from huge plant drums. He stayed in the kids' room on camera all night.
characters.culprit_explanation: He poured leftover machine oil from his demo table down the drain after closing. It matched the factory oil type.

characters.name: Elsa
characters.description: She performs in the winter light show on the plaza. The show uses fog machines and bright lights.
alibis_innocent: She stayed in full costume until late rehearsal ended.
Stage logs show her mic was on when the plant drums moved.
Her prop boxes list only lights and glitter for the show.
alibis_guilty: A tube from her fog machine held the same oil as the river.
She asked how to pour out extra liquid fast behind the stage.
Glitter on a drum matched glitter from her costume.
characters.suspicious_detail: A fog machine hose held the same oil found in the river pipe.
characters.innocent_explanation: The main spill is thick factory machine oil. She was on stage when the plant drums rolled.
characters.culprit_explanation: She poured leftover fog liquid into the drain pipe. It mixed with factory oil already in the pipe.

characters.name: Steve
characters.description: He wears a big square-head mascot suit at river festivals. He is not allowed inside the oil factory.
alibis_innocent: Fans took photos with him far from the plant that night.
The costume head is too big to fit through the drum room door.
His event badge only opens the convention hall doors.
alibis_guilty: Oil from the drums smeared his foam gloves.
He texted a friend about moving heavy props at midnight.
Work clothes in his locker had his wristband stuck in the zipper.
characters.suspicious_detail: River oil smears were found on his foam mascot gloves.
characters.innocent_explanation: The video shows a worker in coveralls. The mascot suit is too wide for the drum room.
characters.culprit_explanation: He took off the suit and wore stolen coveralls. He helped roll drums so no one saw the mascot.

characters.name: SpyderSammy
characters.description: He sprays bug-killing liquid along the riverbank walking path. He keeps the trail clear for visitors.
alibis_innocent: His spray log shows he finished the riverbank route before sunset.
His truck was parked at the yard when the drum video was filmed.
The sprays he uses are river-safe and approved for riverbank work.
alibis_guilty: An empty unlabeled jug by the drain held chemicals he is not allowed to carry.
His route log shows a two-hour gap the night the oil hit the river.
Oily marks on the drain matched an illegal spray bottle in his truck.
characters.suspicious_detail: An empty unlabeled jug by the drain held chemicals he is not allowed to carry.
characters.innocent_explanation: The spill is thick factory machine oil. His allowed sprays are water-safe and do not leave an oil slick.
characters.culprit_explanation: He dumped illegal bug spray into the drain to skip safe disposal fees. It mixed with factory oil already in the pipe.
